problem: Map the helix-cli's role in the Helix Core Infrastructure — how it serves as both a user-facing tool and an in-sandbox agent tool.

files.why: Main CLI entry point — dispatches to subcommands: login, token, inspect, comments, org, tickets, skill, update.
Ticket creation command — demonstrates the API contract for creating tickets with mode (AUTO/BUILD/FIX/RESEARCH/EXECUTE), repos, and description.
Rerun/continue command — POST /api/tickets/{id}/rerun with optional continuationContext. Shows how continuation runs are triggered.
Artifact listing command — shows how step artifacts are retrieved and displayed.
Database inspection command — read-only SQL queries against production databases. Used by agents for runtime evidence.
Log inspection command — search application logs. Used by agents for runtime debugging.
API inspection command — read-only API endpoint calls.
Comment posting command — agents use this to respond to @Helix comments in ticket discussions.
Skill installation command — deploys bundled SKILL.md to agent directories.
HTTP client — RESTful communication with helix-global-server, retry logic (3 attempts, exponential backoff), auth headers.
Configuration management — ~/.hlx/config.json with multi-org support, token storage, auto-update settings.
Package metadata — published as @projectxinnovation/helix-cli, zero runtime dependencies, binary 'hlx'.

facts: The CLI binary is 'hlx' — installed globally via npm as @projectxinnovation/helix-cli.
The CLI is installed INSIDE the Vercel Sandbox as a dependency of sandbox-runtime-assets/workflow-steps/package.json (@projectxinnovation/helix-cli ^1.2.0).
Inside the sandbox, hlx is symlinked to PATH at /home/vercel-sandbox/.local/bin/hlx by the orchestrator's configureInspectionForStep function.
Agents use hlx for runtime inspection: 'hlx inspect db' for SQL queries, 'hlx inspect logs' for log searches, 'hlx inspect api' for API calls.
Agents use 'hlx comments post' to respond to user comments tagged with @Helix.
The CLI authenticates via HELIX_INSPECT_TOKEN and HELIX_INSPECT_BASE_URL environment variables when running inside a sandbox, or via ~/.hlx/config.json for user-facing usage.
The CLI has zero runtime dependencies — only TypeScript as a dev dependency. All HTTP communication is done with Node.js built-in fetch.
Ticket references can be specified as internal ID, short ID (e.g., BLD-339), or ticket number (e.g., 339).
The CLI supports multi-org context switching with org current/list/switch commands.
unknowns: Whether the CLI's skill install command is used by the orchestrator during sandbox setup or only by external users.
The full content of the bundled SKILL.md and what capabilities it advertises to external agents.
